description:
You are a US Coast Guard Maritime Safety Analyst conducting a climatological assessment of the Bering Sea to brief the winter commercial fishing fleet on 'superstructure icing' (freezing spray) risks. Freezing spray occurs when very cold air blows over unfrozen water. Analyze the January climatologies for sea surface temperature (SST) from ~/PanoplyData/sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc and surface air temperature from ~/PanoplyData/air.mon.ltm.nc. Export plots of the January temperatures for the Bering Sea/Alaska region to ~/Documents/MaritimeSafety/bering_sst_jan.png and ~/Documents/MaritimeSafety/bering_airtemp_jan.png. Estimate the typical January air and sea surface temperatures in Celsius for the central open-water Bering Sea and record them in a report at ~/Documents/MaritimeSafety/icing_risk_report.txt using the following format:

TARGET_BASIN: Bering Sea
ANALYSIS_MONTH: January
APPROX_AIR_TEMP_C: [Your estimate of the central Bering Sea air temp in Celsius]
APPROX_SST_C: [Your estimate of the central Bering Sea water temp in Celsius]
HAZARD_TYPE: Freezing Spray
RISK_LEVEL: HIGH

metadata.occupation: US Coast Guard Maritime Safety Analyst
